title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Mets 6-2, securing their seventh consecutive win on Friday.
Sonny Gray earned his 11th win of the season, pitching six innings of one-run ball against the Mets.
Anthony Seigler hit a two-run homer, contributing significantly to Boston's victory despite their travel delays.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez criticized reporter Greg Bedard on social media for comments about his physicality as an NFL corner.
Bedard claimed some within the Patriots organization doubt Gonzalez's physical presence on the field.
Gonzalez recorded 69 tackles in 2025, contrasting slight discrepancies with more physical corners in the NFL.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict Red Sox may select outfielder Chase Brunson, right-handed pitcher Taylor Rabe, or third baseman Bo Lowrance in 2026 MLB Draft.
Chase Brunson, from TCU, is a 6-foot-3 right-handed outfielder known for his power-hitting and solid on-base performance.
Taylor Rabe, an Ole Miss pitcher, has a fastball reaching triple-digits and led his team to the College World Series.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's new adaptation of 'Little House on the Prairie' offers a wholesome take on the Ingalls family story.
'The Long Walk,' based on a Stephen King novel, is now streaming on HBO Max, featuring a dystopian competition.
Peacock's 'The Five-Star Weekend,' filmed on Nantucket, stars Jennifer Garner as a celebrity chef dealing with grief.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela of the Boston Red Sox replaces Aaron Judge as a 2026 MLB All-Star.
Rafaela is hitting .283 with eight home runs and 39 RBIs over 87 games this season.
He joins three other Red Sox players, Willson Contreras, Ranger Suarez, and Aroldis Chapman, as 2026 All-Stars.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut will compete in the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship at The Brook Casino in Seabrook, NH.
Chestnut holds a personal record of 242 chicken wings in 10 minutes but Geoffrey Esper holds the all-time record with 281 wings.
The Saugus Wing Eating Championship is named after Kowloon's signature wings, coated in sticky Asian-style barbecue sauce.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Boston hosted seven World Cup matches, concluding with France's 2-0 win over Morocco in Foxborough.
Kylian Mbappé scored his eighth tournament goal, contributing to France's victory before exiting with an injury.
The event boosted Boston's global profile, highlighted by fan stories and iconic celebrations from Scotland and Norway.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola faces charges for allegedly attacking friend Marianna DiMercurio with a WD-40-soaked rag in Gloucester.
The attack followed a dispute over $160,000 Palazzola allegedly owed DiMercurio from a previous loan.
Palazzola has been indicted on multiple charges and is scheduled for arraignment on July 21.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Coco Fernandez, owner of Mobar in Waltham, became a barber for France's national soccer team through a WhatsApp message.
Fernandez has cut hair for soccer stars like Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé during France's World Cup stay in Boston.
Security checks were extensive as Fernandez and his team worked with athletes, highlighting a new level of client experience.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox faced a 17-hour delay leaving Chicago due to multiple plane issues before their game against the Mets.
The team's flight from Chicago was initially scheduled for 9:45 p.m. ET Thursday but departed at 3 p.m. ET Friday.
MLB delayed the start of Friday's game to 7:50 p.m., allowing the Red Sox to arrive in New York just in time.
